name: getWebpage
description:
Fetch a webpage and extract its readable text in pages. The first call returns the first chunk; if has_more=true, call again with start_ctx_id=next_start_ctx_id to get the next chunk — repeat until has_more=false. The optional 'prompt' parameter biases the summary without overriding facts.

PAGINATION: Every response includes total_pages (total number of pages), current_page (current page number, 1-based), and page_size (characters per page). You can compute all page start offsets as multiples of page_size and dispatch them as parallel workers in one batch.
policyHint: When the user provides a specific URL or when search results need deeper reading from a source, fetch the page directly. If has_more=true in the response, always call again with start_ctx_id=next_start_ctx_id to retrieve remaining page content before answering.

prompt.description: OPTIONAL: extra system instructions to bias the summary (e.g., 'focus on statements by user X'). Used only in summary mode; ignored in dump mode.

userPrompt.description: User question or instruction to execute against the page text (required — this is the main task).

start_ctx_id.description: Pagination offset (character position). Omit or set to 0 for the first page. Use the next_start_ctx_id value from the previous response to fetch the next chunk, or compute as (page_number - 1) * page_size for parallel fetching.
